completeness.rationale: The triage extracted most expected information: crash on manual save (toolbar click), related to imported CSV with special characters (em-dashes, curly quotes), started after CSV import, and encoding-related error message. However, it missed the size-dependent aspect — the ground truth reveals the crash only happens with large lists (200+ tasks / >64KB payload) and small lists (<50 tasks) save fine even with imported data. The agent noted the ~200 task count but didn't probe whether list size was a factor. It also failed to collect the exact version (v2.3.1) and platform (macOS 14.2), which the reporter knew.

accuracy.rationale: The root cause hypothesis is close — it correctly identifies encoding issues with special characters during save serialization. However, it incorrectly attributes the encoding to Windows-1252 (cp1252) when the actual issue is non-ASCII characters in UTF-8 combined with a payload size threshold (64KB). The hypothesis misses the size-dependent nature of the bug entirely. The general direction (encoding + special chars + save path) is correct, but a developer following the cp1252 theory might look in the wrong place initially.

efficiency.rationale: The conversation completed in just 2 agent questions plus a final summary — very efficient. Both questions were well-targeted and drew out key information. The only inefficiency is that the agent could have asked about version/platform in the first round, and could have probed the size dependency in the second round instead of jumping to resolution.

question_quality.rationale: The first question effectively covered what/how/when in a structured way, drawing out the CSV import timing and encoding error clue. The second question was a good diagnostic test (try saving a small plain-text list) and correctly probed the CSV source. However, the agent didn't ask about the exact app version or OS, and didn't probe whether the crash was size-dependent (e.g., 'does it crash if you remove some of the imported tasks?'), which would have revealed the 64KB threshold.

actionability.rationale: The summary includes clear reproduction steps, a reasonable root cause hypothesis, and a concrete recommended fix covering both import sanitization and save error handling. A developer could start investigating the save serialization path immediately. However, the missing size-dependency insight means a developer might not discover the 64KB threshold without additional investigation, and the cp1252 red herring could waste some initial debugging time.

notable_strengths: Excellent efficiency — resolved in just 2 turns while extracting substantial detail
Strong diagnostic reasoning that correctly connected the CSV import timing to the encoding error
Well-structured first question that covered multiple dimensions without overwhelming the reporter
Actionable summary with concrete reproduction steps and multi-layered fix recommendation
notable_weaknesses: Failed to discover the size-dependent nature of the crash (only triggers above ~64KB payload)
Did not collect exact version (v2.3.1) or platform (macOS 14.2) despite listing these as information gaps
Root cause hypothesis incorrectly specifies Windows-1252 encoding rather than the actual UTF-8 serialization + size threshold issue
Jumped to resolution after 2 turns without probing whether removing imported tasks or reducing list size was a workaround
most_insightful_question: Could you try creating a brand-new task list with just a couple of simple tasks (plain English text, no special characters) and saving that? — This diagnostic test efficiently confirmed the issue was tied to the imported data, though it could have been even better by also asking whether removing some imported tasks from the large list helped.
missed_opportunities: What version of TaskFlow are you on, and what OS? (would have captured macOS 14.2 and v2.3.1)
If you remove the imported tasks from your big list, does saving work again? (would have confirmed the imported data is the trigger)
Does the crash still happen if you import only a few rows from the CSV into a smaller list? (would have revealed the size-dependent threshold)
